amendment_scope: Separate capture-phase, terminal, and physical-growth telemetry; add an explicit capture gate; and audit through a read-only connection. Wire, event-time, causal depth-band, market, target, and model semantics remain unchanged.

failure_evidence.measurement_limitation: The v5 process endpoint followed terminal finalization, in-process audit, aggregation queries, and latency reconstruction, so it did not isolate capture-phase writes.

inheritance.rule: All v1 through v5 logical requirements remain binding except for the v6 run schema, report schema, telemetry attribution, and gate amendments below.

market_and_calendar_scope.venue: Binance USD-M perpetual public market data
market_and_calendar_scope.utc_day_semantics: partition and resampling boundary only, never a crypto market close
market_and_calendar_scope.listed_etf_or_security_semantics: A listed product is a separate instrument and must use its actual venue calendar, including holidays, early closes, halts, and auctions. Its close never creates a Binance crypto close.

capture_phase_telemetry.start: after preflight and run metadata creation, immediately before the writer thread opens its DuckDB connection
capture_phase_telemetry.end: immediately after the writer thread joins and its DuckDB connection has closed, before segment or run finalization, audit, aggregation queries, latency reconstruction, or report persistence

database_physical_growth_telemetry.start: DuckDB plus WAL bytes immediately before the writer starts
database_physical_growth_telemetry.end: DuckDB plus WAL bytes immediately after the writer connection closes

terminal_phase_telemetry.start: after the capture endpoint is sealed
terminal_phase_telemetry.end: after write finalization, read-only audit, aggregation queries, and latency reconstruction but before report persistence
terminal_phase_telemetry.reason: It is reported independently so terminal work cannot contaminate capture-phase storage attribution.

duckdb_policy.decision: A 44,506-message real full-table benchmark did not support the 512 MiB candidate.
